RulePack.Name: CERT Secure Coding Rulepack, C++ language (combined)
RulePack.Description: CERT Secure Coding Rulepack, C++ language (combined)

SemanticRule.VulnCategory: RES32-C. Use new and delete rather than raw memory allocation and deallocation

Description.Abstract: RES32-C. Use new and delete rather than raw memory allocation and deallocation
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C++ Secure Coding Rule RES32-C. More information can be found in RES32-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in RES32-C.

Description.Abstract: RES39-C. Do not use longjmp
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C++ Secure Coding Rule RES39-C. More information can be found in RES39-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in RES39-C.

Description.Abstract: DAN32-C. Do not delete this
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C++ Secure Coding Rule DAN32-C. More information can be found in DAN32-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in DAN32-C.

SemanticRule.VulnCategory: BSC30-C. Use the c_str() member to retrieve a const pointer to a null-terminated byte string

Description.Abstract: BSC30-C. Use the c_str() member to retrieve a const pointer to a null-terminated byte string
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C++ Secure Coding Rule BSC30-C. More information can be found in BSC30-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in BSC30-C.

SemanticRule.VulnCategory: EXP04-A. Do not perform byte-by-byte comparisons between structures

SemanticRule.Notes: Note that the information for this C++ rule is slightly different than the corresponding C rule with the same name.
SemanticRule.VulnCategory: INT06-A. Use strtol() to convert a string token to an integer

Description.Abstract: INT06-A. Use strtol() to convert a string token to an integer
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C++ Secure Coding Recommendation INT06-A. More information can be found in INT06-A.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in INT06-A.

ControlflowRule.VulnCategory: FLP32-C. Prevent or detect domain and range errors in math functions

Description.Abstract: FLP32-C. Prevent or detect domain and range errors in math functions
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Rule FLP32-C. More information can be found in FLP32-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in FLP32-C.

ControlflowRule.VulnCategory: DAN34-C. Do not dereference invalid pointers

Description.Abstract: DAN34-C. Do not dereference invalid pointers
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C++ Secure Coding Rule DAN34-C. More information can be found in DAN34-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in DAN34-C.

ControlflowRule.VulnCategory: BSC31-C. Do not modify the null-terminated byte string returned by the c_str() member

Description.Abstract: BSC31-C. Do not modify the null-terminated byte string returned by the c_str() member
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C++ Secure Coding Rule BSC31-C. More information can be found in BSC31-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in BSC31-C.

ControlflowRule.VulnCategory: BSC33-C. Use valid references, pointers, and iterators to reference string objects

Description.Abstract: BSC33-C. Use valid references, pointers, and iterators to reference string objects
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C++ Secure Coding Rule BSC33-C. More information can be found in BSC33-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in BSC33-C.

ControlflowRule.VulnCategory: BSC32-C. Do not use the pointer value returned by c_str() after any subsequent call to a non-const member function

Description.Abstract: BSC32-C. Do not use the pointer value returned by c_str() after any subsequent call to a non-const member function
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C++ Secure Coding Rule BSC32-C. More information can be found in BSC32-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in BSC32-C.

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: ARR30-C. Guarantee that array indices are within the valid range

Description.Abstract: ARR30-C. Guarantee that array indices are within the valid range
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C++ Secure Coding Rule ARR30-C. More information can be found in ARR30-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in ARR30-C.

StructuralRule.Predicate: Operation: op == "+" and lhs is [VariableAccess: variable.referenceTaken] and rhs is [VariableAccess: variable.type.name matches "(signed)?int"]

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: EXP33-C. Do not reference uninitialized variables

Description.Abstract: EXP33-C. Do not reference uninitialized variables
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C++ Secure Coding Rule EXP33-C. More information can be found in EXP33-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in EXP33-C.

Description.Abstract: DAN32-C. Do not delete this
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C++ Secure Coding Rule DAN32-C. More information can be found in DAN32-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in DAN32-C.

StructuralRule.Predicate: FunctionCall : function.name matches "operator delete" and arguments[0] is [VariableAccess : variable.name is "this~"]

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: OBJ33-C. Do not slice polymorphic objects

Description.Abstract: OBJ33-C. Do not slice polymorphic objects
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C++ Secure Coding Rule OBJ33-C. More information can be found in OBJ33-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in OBJ33-C.

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: DCL30-C. Do not use names reserved for the implementation

Description.Abstract: DCL30-C. Do not use names reserved for the implementation
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C++ Secure Coding Rule DCL30-C. More information can be found in DCL30-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in DCL30-C.

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: EXP36-C. Do not cast or delete pointers to incomplete classes

Description.Abstract: EXP36-C. Do not cast or delete pointers to incomplete classes
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C++ Secure Coding Rule EXP36-C. More information can be found in EXP36-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in EXP36-C.

StructuralRule.Predicate: AssignmentStatement a: a.lhs.type.name is "[UnknownType]" and is [not( a.rhs.type.name is "[UnkownType]")]

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: EXP37-C. Avoid side effects in assertions

Description.Abstract: EXP37-C. Avoid side effects in assertions
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C++ Secure Coding Rule EXP37-C. More information can be found in EXP37-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in EXP37-C.

StructuralRule.Predicate: FunctionCall : is [not (enclosingFunction.name is "main")] and function.name is "__eprintf" and arguments[0] is [StringLiteral : image matches ".*assertion.*"] and arguments[3] is [StringLiteral : image matches ".*\+\+.*" or image matches ".*\..*\(\).*" ]

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: INT31-C. Ensure that integer conversions do not result in lost or misinterpreted data

Description.Abstract: INT31-C. Ensure that integer conversions do not result in lost or misinterpreted data
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C++ Secure Coding Rule INT31-C. More information can be found in INT31-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in INT31-C.

StructuralRule.Predicate: AssignmentStatement a: a.lhs is [ VariableAccess va: va.variable.type.name matches "(unsigned|signed)?\s*(int|char|short|long)" and a.rhs is [VariableAccess vb: vb.variable.type.name matches "(unsigned)?\s*(long|int|short|char)" and is [not (vb.variable.type.name == va.variable.type.name) ] and not a.enclosingFunction contains [IfStatement : expression is [Operation : lhs is [VariableAccess : variable.name == vb.variable.name] or rhs is [VariableAccess : variable.name == vb.variable.name ] ] ] ] ]

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: OBJ33-C. Do not slice polymorphic objects

Description.Abstract: OBJ33-C. Do not slice polymorphic objects
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C++ Secure Coding Rule OBJ33-C. More information can be found in OBJ33-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in OBJ33-C.

StructuralRule.Predicate: AssignmentStatement as: (not as.lhs.type.primitive) and (not as.rhs.type.primitive) and (as.lhs.type.definition.abstract)

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: INT33-C. Ensure that division and modulo operations do not result in divide-by-zero errors

Description.Abstract: INT33-C. Ensure that division and modulo operations do not result in divide-by-zero errors
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C++ Secure Coding Rule INT33-C. More information can be found in INT33-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in INT33-C.

StructuralRule.Predicate: AssignmentStatement a: a.rhs is [Operation o: o.op matches "(\/|\%)" and o.rhs is [VariableAccess vb: vb.variable.type.name matches "(unsigned)?\s*(long|int|short|char)" and not a.enclosingFunction contains [IfStatement : expression is [Operation : lhs is [VariableAccess : variable.name == vb.variable.name] ] ] ] and o.lhs is [VariableAccess vc: vc.variable.type.name matches "(unsigned)?\s*(long|int|short|char)" and not a.enclosingFunction contains [IfStatement : expression is [Operation : lhs is [VariableAccess : variable.name == vc.variable.name] ] ] ] ]

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: INT32-C. Ensure that integer operations do not result in an overflow

Description.Abstract: INT32-C. Ensure that integer operations do not result in an overflow
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C++ Secure Coding Rule INT32-C. More information can be found in INT32-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in INT32-C.

StructuralRule.Predicate: AssignmentStatement a: a.rhs is [Operation o: o.op matches "(\+|-|\*|\/|<<|>>)" and o.rhs is [VariableAccess vb: vb.variable.type.name matches "(unsigned)?\s*(long|int|short|char)" and not a.enclosingFunction contains [IfStatement : expression is [Operation : lhs is [VariableAccess : variable.name == vb.variable.name] ] ] ] and o.lhs is [VariableAccess vc: vc.variable.type.name matches "(unsigned)?\s*(long|int|short|char)" and not a.enclosingFunction contains [IfStatement : expression is [Operation : lhs is [VariableAccess : variable.name == vc.variable.name] ] ] ] ] and not a in [WhileStatement: ]

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: INT32-C. Ensure that integer operations do not result in an overflow

Description.Abstract: INT32-C. Ensure that integer operations do not result in an overflow
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C++ Secure Coding Rule INT32-C. More information can be found in INT32-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in INT32-C.

StructuralRule.Predicate: AssignmentStatement a: a.rhs is [Operation o: o.op matches "-" and o.rhs is [VariableAccess vb: vb.variable.type.name matches "(unsigned)?\s*(long|int|short|char)" and not a.enclosingFunction contains [IfStatement : expression is [Operation : lhs is [VariableAccess : variable.name == vb.variable.name] ] ] ] ] and not a in [WhileStatement: ]

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: INT32-C. Ensure that integer operations do not result in an overflow

Description.Abstract: INT32-C. Ensure that integer operations do not result in an overflow
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C++ Secure Coding Rule INT32-C. More information can be found in INT32-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in INT32-C.

StructuralRule.Predicate: AssignmentStatement a: a.rhs is [Operation o: o.op matches "(\+|-)" and o.lhs is [VariableAccess vc: vc.variable.type.name matches "(unsigned)?\s*(long|int|short|char)" and not a.enclosingFunction contains [IfStatement : expression is [Operation : lhs is [VariableAccess : variable.name == vc.variable.name] ] ] ] ] and not a in [WhileStatement: ]

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: FLP31-C. Do not use floating point variables as loop counters

Description.Abstract: FLP31-C. Do not use floating point variables as loop counters
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C++ Secure Coding Rule FLP31-C. More information can be found in FLP31-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in FLP31-C.

StructuralRule.Predicate: WhileStatement w: w.expression is [Operation : lhs is [VariableAccess vb: vb.variable.type.name == "float" and w.block contains [Operation : op matches "(\+|-|\*|\/|<<|>>)" and lhs is [VariableAccess: variable.name == vb.variable.name] ] ] ]

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: INT06-A. Use strtol() to convert a string token to an integer

Description.Abstract: INT06-A. Use strtol() to convert a string token to an integer
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C++ Secure Coding Recommendation INT06-A. More information can be found in INT06-A.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in INT06-A.

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: FLP30-C. Take granularity into account when comparing floating point values

Description.Abstract: FLP30-C. Take granularity into account when comparing floating point values
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C++ Secure Coding Rule FLP30-C. More information can be found in FLP30-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in FLP30-C.

StructuralRule.Predicate: IfStatement : expression is [Operation : op == "==" and rhs.type.name == "float" and lhs.type.name == "float" ]

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: INT35-C. Do not truncate the return value from a character input function

Description.Abstract: INT35-C. Do not truncate the return value from a character input function
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C++ Secure Coding Rule INT35-C. More information can be found in INT35-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in INT35-C.

StructuralRule.Predicate: AssignmentStatement : lhs is [VariableAccess va: va.type.name matches "char" ] and rhs is [FunctionCall : function.returnType.name matches "int" ]

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: BSC32-C. Do not use the pointer value returned by c_str() after any subsequent call to a non-const member function

Description.Abstract: BSC32-C. Do not use the pointer value returned by c_str() after any subsequent call to a non-const member function
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C++ Secure Coding Rule BSC32-C. More information can be found in BSC32-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in BSC32-C.

StructuralRule.Predicate: AssignmentStatement : rhs is [FunctionCall : function.name is "c_str" and instance is [VariableAccess va: enclosingFunction contains [FunctionCall : instance is [VariableAccess vb: vb.variable.name == va.variable.name ] and function.name matches "(append|assign|clear|erase|insert|push_back|rbegin|rend|replace|reserve|resize)" ] ] ]
